1: In that day shall this song be sung in the land of Judah; We have a strong city; salvation will God God appoint for for walls and bulwarks.
2: Open ye the gates, that the righteous nation which keepeth the truth may enter in.
3: Thou wilt keep him him in perfect peace, whose whose mind is is stayed on thee: on thee: because he trusteth in thee.
4: Trust ye in the LORD LORD for ever: for in the LORD LORD JEHOVAH is is everlasting strength:
5: for he bringeth down them that dwell on high; the lofty city, he layeth it low; he layeth it low, even even to the ground; he bringeth it even even to the dust.
6: The foot shall tread it down, even even the feet of the poor, and and the steps of the needy.
7: The way of the just is is uprightness: thou, most upright, dost weigh the path of the just.
8: Yea, in the way of thy judgments, O LORD LORD , have we waited for thee; the desire of our our soul is is to thy name, and to the remembrance of thee.
9: With my soul have I desired thee in the night; yea, with my spirit within me will I seek thee early: for when thy judgments are are in the earth, the inhabitants of the world will learn righteousness.
10: Let favour be shewed to the wicked, yet yet will he not learn righteousness: in the land of uprightness will he deal unjustly, and will not behold the majesty of the LORD LORD .
11: LORD LORD , when when thy hand is lifted up, they will not see: but but they shall see, and be ashamed for their their envy at the people; yea, the fire of thine enemies shall devour them.
12: LORD LORD , thou wilt ordain peace for us: for thou also hast wrought all our works in us.
13: O LORD LORD our God, other other lords beside thee have had dominion over us: but but by thee only will we make mention of thy name.
14: They are They are dead, they shall not live; they are they are deceased, they shall not rise: therefore hast thou visited and destroyed them, and made all their memory to perish.
15: Thou hast increased the nation, O LORD LORD , thou hast increased the nation: thou art glorified: thou hadst removed it it far unto unto all the ends of the earth.
16: LORD LORD , in trouble have they visited thee, they poured out a prayer when when thy chastening was was upon them.
17: Like as a woman with child, that that draweth near the time of her delivery, is in pain, and and crieth out in her pangs; so have we been in thy sight, O LORD LORD .
18: We have been with child, we have been in pain, we have as it were brought forth wind; we have not wrought any deliverance in the earth; neither have the inhabitants of the world fallen.
19: Thy dead men men shall live, together with together with my dead body shall they arise. Awake and sing, ye that dwell in dust: for thy dew is as is as the dew of herbs, and the earth shall cast out the dead.
20: Come, my people, enter thou into thy chambers, and shut thy doors about thee: hide thyself as it were for a little moment, until the indignation be overpast.
21: For, behold, the LORD LORD cometh out of his place to punish the inhabitants of the earth for their iniquity: the earth also shall disclose her blood, and shall no more cover her slain.
